name: Los barrancos de la Galera - Circular route from Mas de Barberans
name_original: Los barrancos de la Galera - Vuelta circular desde Mas de Barberans
region_intro: Jogging around Mas de Barberans offers diverse terrain within the Montsià region of Tarragona, characterized by its mountainous orography and rich agricultural landscapes. The area is nestled at the foot of the Els Ports massif, featuring extensive olive groves, ravines, and forests. A significant portion of the municipal territory is integrated into the Els Ports Natural Park, providing varied geological formations and unique natural features for running. Best jogging routes around Mas de Barberans The most popular jogging route is Los barrancos de la Galera - Circular route from Mas de Barberans, a 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant elevation gain amidst scenic barrancos (ravines). Another top favourite among local runners is Castell de l'Airosa loop from Mas de Barberans, a difficult 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route offers mountain trails and views, typically completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Local runners also love the Cova d'en Marc – Castell de l'Airosa loop from Castell de l'Airosa, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of Els Ports Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Jogging around Mas de Barberans is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive olive groves, and the protected areas of Els Ports Natural Park, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Mas de Barberans are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Mas de Barberans's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of running routes around Mas de Barberans?
A: The running routes in Mas de Barberans are predominantly challenging. Out of 16 available routes, 15 are rated as difficult, with only one moderate option. This is due to the region's mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, particularly within the Els Ports Natural Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Mas de Barberans?
A: Yes, Mas de Barberans offers several long-distance running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951080074/los-barrancos-de-la-galera-circular-route-from-mas-de-barberans" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los barrancos de la Galera - Circular route from Mas de Barberans&lt;/a&gt; covers 17.8 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. A particularly extensive option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Airosa – Cova d'en Marc loop from Mas de Barberans&lt;/a&gt;, which stretches over 31 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while running in Mas de Barberans?
A: Jogging in Mas de Barberans offers stunning natural beauty. You can expect to see vast olive groves, diverse geological formations within the Els Ports Natural Park, and panoramic views encompassing the Ebro Delta and distant mountain ranges. Notable attractions include the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by ancient sites like the Cocó de la Gralla Rock Paintings.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region around Mas de Barberans features both a beautiful waterfall and a reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a picturesque 30-meter cascade, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers magnificent views with its varying water colors. While specific running routes directly passing these may vary, they are significant natural attractions in the vicinity that can be incorporated into your running plans or explored separately.

Q: Can I find routes with panoramic views of the Ebro Delta or the surrounding mountains?
A: Absolutely. Mas de Barberans is nestled at the foot of the Els Ports massif, offering privileged geographical locations for breathtaking panoramic views. Many trails, especially those with higher elevation, provide stunning vistas that include the 'sea of olive trees,' the Ebro Delta, and the distant mountain ranges of Cardó, Montsià, Godall, and even the castle of Peñíscola on clear days. The Cursa l'Airosa mountain race, for instance, is known for spectacular views from Peñíscola to the Ebro Delta.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Mas de Barberans?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Mas de Barberans are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951080074/los-barrancos-de-la-galera-circular-route-from-mas-de-barberans" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los barrancos de la Galera - Circular route from Mas de Barberans&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Airosa loop from Mas de Barberans&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices among local runners.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mas de Barberans?
A: The running routes in Mas de Barberans are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 5 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Els Ports Natural Park, and the challenging yet rewarding mountain trails that offer spectacular views.

Q: Is Mas de Barberans suitable for dog-friendly running?
A: While the Els Ports Natural Park, which covers a significant portion of Mas de Barberans, generally welcomes dogs, it's important to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check specific park regulations or trail signs for any restrictions, especially in sensitive ecological areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Mas de Barberans?
A: The best time to go running in Mas de Barberans is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making it more comfortable for longer runs and exploring the mountainous terrain. Summer can be very hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable if visiting during this season. Winters are generally mild but can be cooler at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any less crowded running trails in Mas de Barberans?
A: Given that Mas de Barberans is nestled within the extensive Els Ports Natural Park and is part of the Terres de l'Ebre Biosphere Reserve, many trails offer a sense of solitude and natural immersion. While popular routes like the 'Los barrancos de la Galera' can see more activity, the vast network of paths through olive groves and within the natural park means you can often find quieter sections, especially on weekdays or by exploring lesser-known loops.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes in Mas de Barberans are rated as difficult due to significant elevation, families looking for easier options might consider shorter, flatter sections through the extensive olive groves surrounding the village. These paths offer a gentler terrain and a chance to enjoy the agricultural landscape. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it's appropriate for your family's fitness level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Mas de Barberans?
A: The running routes in Mas de Barberans offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter paths winding through traditional olive groves, which are generally less challenging. However, a significant portion of the routes are within the Els Ports Natural Park, featuring mountainous orography, ravines (barrancos), and forest trails. Expect a mix of rocky paths, dirt tracks, and some steeper ascents and descents, providing a challenging and varied experience.
tips: Airosa Castle is a rock formation that looks like a castle construction. It is located in Ombria del Castell, a valley with a group of pinnacles of different sizes.
